days.title: Narrating my day: what I did

vocab.exampleEn: In the morning I drink coffee.
I had breakfast with bread and cheese.

grammar.title: The past tense (aorist): talking about what I did
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian uses the aorist past tense to describe completed actions. For 'I', many verbs end in -х: станах (I got up), закусих (I had breakfast), отидох (I went). Notice there are no noun cases — relations come from prepositions like на (to/at) and с (with). Just say the verb plus a preposition where needed: отидох на работа = I went to work.

examples.english: I got up at seven o'clock.
Afterwards I had breakfast.
I went to work by bus.

reading.title: Моята сутрин
reading.text: Станах рано. После закусих с хляб и сирене. Сутрин пия кафе, а днес отидох на работа.
reading.translation: I got up early. Afterwards I had breakfast with bread and cheese. In the morning I drink coffee, and today I went to work.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe what you did this morning using the past tense. Say at least three things: when you got up, what you had for breakfast, and where you went. Use сутрин and после.

days.title: What happened next: после and тогава

vocab.exampleEn: Afterwards we went to the park.
First we ate, then we left.
I met her on the street.
The street is long and quiet.

grammar.title: Ordering events with после and тогава
grammar.explanation: To put events in order, use sequence words at the start of the sentence. първо (first), после (afterwards/then), тогава (then, at that point). They simply sit before the verb and do not change any word endings. Combine with aorist verbs: Първо станах, после закусих = First I got up, then I had breakfast.

examples.english: First I went to work.
Afterwards I met a friend.
Then I saw that it was late.

reading.title: На улицата
reading.text: Първо тръгнах за работа. После срещнах един приятел на улицата. Тогава видях, че е много късно.
reading.translation: First I set off for work. Afterwards I met a friend on the street. Then I saw that it was very late.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell a short story about meeting someone. Order your three sentences with първо, после, and тогава. Use срещнах or видях.

days.title: He, she and we did: more past forms

vocab.exampleEn: He said that he is coming.

grammar.title: Past tense for he, she, we and they
grammar.explanation: In the aorist, third person singular (той/тя/то) often drops the -х ending: каза (said), дойде (came), отиде (went). For 'we' add -хме: ядохме (we ate), отидохме (we went). For 'they' add -ха: отидоха (they went), казаха (they said). The pronoun (той, тя, ние, те) is optional — the verb ending already shows who acts.

examples.russian: Тя дойде вчера и ние ядохме заедно.
examples.english: She came yesterday and we ate together.

examples.russian: Той каза, че те отидоха на море.
examples.english: He said that they went to the seaside.
We worked together all day.

reading.title: Вчера заедно
reading.text: Тя дойде вчера. После ние ядохме заедно. Той каза, че е хубав ден.
reading.translation: She came yesterday. Afterwards we ate together. He said that it is a nice day.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe something you and others did yesterday. Use at least one 'we' form (ядохме, отидохме) and one 'she/he' form (дойде, каза). Add вчера and заедно.

days.title: Life events: the big moments

vocab.exampleEn: I was born in the winter.
I moved to a new city.

grammar.title: Telling life events with the verb 'to be' and the past
grammar.explanation: Unlike Russian, Bulgarian has a present 'to be': съм, си, е, сме, сте, са. Use it to state facts: Роден съм в София = I am born (was born) in Sofia. The adjective роден agrees: роден (masc.), родена (fem.). For dated events use the aorist plus the preposition през (during/in): Родих се през 1990 година. No noun cases — use prepositions в (in), през (during).

examples.english: I was born in a small town.

examples.russian: Учих в София, после се преместих.
examples.english: I studied in Sofia, then I moved.
My life is interesting.

reading.title: Моят живот
reading.text: Родих се в малък град. После учих в София. Тогава се преместих в нов град.
reading.translation: I was born in a small town. Afterwards I studied in Sofia. Then I moved to a new city.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell three facts about your life using the past tense: where you were born, where you studied, and one more event. Use роден съм / родих се and после.

days.title: Finishing the story: накрая

vocab.exampleEn: In the end everything was fine.
This is the end of the story.
I am content with the day.

grammar.title: Closing a story with накрая
grammar.explanation: накрая (in the end, finally) marks the conclusion of a story. Put it at the start of the last sentence: Накрая се върнах вкъщи = In the end I returned home. To say how you felt, use 'to be' plus an adjective that agrees: уморен съм (I am tired, masc.), доволна съм (I am content, fem.). The full event order becomes: първо… после… тогава… накрая.

examples.english: In the end I returned home.
I was tired and fell asleep right away.
In the end I was very content.

reading.title: Краят на деня
reading.text: Накрая се върнах вкъщи. Бях уморен и заспах веднага. Всичко беше наред.
reading.translation: In the end I returned home. I was tired and fell asleep right away. Everything was alright.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell the ending of your day's story. Use накрая for the last event, say where you returned, and describe how you felt with уморен or доволен.

days.title: A whole story from start to finish

vocab.exampleEn: I forgot the key at home.

grammar.title: Putting it all together: a full narration
grammar.explanation: Now combine everything: open the scene, use sequence words to order events, add изведнъж (suddenly) for a surprise, and close with накрая. A typical chain: Първо… После… Изведнъж… Тогава… Накрая. Keep using aorist verbs and the present 'to be' for feelings. Remember: no cases — prepositions (в, на, с, за) and word order carry the meaning.

examples.english: I remember a funny incident.

examples.russian: Изведнъж забравих какво искам да кажа.
examples.english: Suddenly I forgot what I want to say.
In the end we all laughed.

reading.title: Смешна случка
reading.text: Помня една смешна случка. Първо забравих ключа, после изведнъж заваля дъжд. Накрая разбрах, че денят е смешен.
reading.translation: I remember a funny incident. First I forgot the key, then suddenly it started to rain. In the end I understood that the day is funny.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell a complete short story (four or five sentences) about a memorable day. Use първо, после, изведнъж, and накрая, and at least four past-tense verbs.

days.title: Review: telling a story

grammar.title: Recap: past tense and ordering events
grammar.explanation: This week you learned the aorist past tense: -х for 'I' (станах, отидох, видях), often no ending for he/she (каза, дойде), -хме for 'we' (ядохме), -ха for 'they' (отидоха). You ordered events with първо, после, тогава, изведнъж, and накрая, and used the present 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са) with agreeing adjectives (уморен, доволна) for feelings. Remember: Bulgarian has no noun cases — use prepositions (в, на, с, за, през) and word order. The definite article is a suffix: ден→денят, история→историята.

examples.english: First I got up, then I had breakfast.

examples.russian: Тя дойде и ние ядохме заедно.
examples.english: She came and we ate together.
In the end I returned home tired.

reading.title: Разказ за деня
reading.text: — Какво се случи вчера? — Първо станах рано и закусих. После отидох на работа и срещнах приятел. Накрая се върнах вкъщи доволен.
reading.translation: — What happened yesterday? — First I got up early and had breakfast. Afterwards I went to work and met a friend. In the end I returned home content.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell the full story of yesterday from morning to night. Use at least six past-tense verbs and all four sequence words (първо, после, тогава, накрая). End by saying how you felt using съм plus an adjective.
